title: Set Up a Replacement Scanner Using Barcode Interface
summary: Configure a replacement Zebra DS3608-SR scanner using a Windows laptop and Barcode Interface application by confirming required hardware and network connectivity, connecting and charging the scanner dock, logging in with a FORTNA account, checking connectivity status, accessing settings, verifying prefixes, updating HTTP and OPC URLs with the documented control IP addresses, and saving the settings.
when_to_use: Use when a scanner has been replaced and the new scanner needs to be set up using the Barcode Interface application.

responsibilities: Prepare the required laptop, dock, cables, power, and network connectivity.
Connect and power the scanner dock and scanner.
Log into the laptop and use Barcode Interface.
Calibrate the scanner if the connectivity indicator is red.
Access scanner settings, verify prefixes, update HTTP and OPC URLs, and save settings.
Escalate when required IP addresses are unavailable, connectivity cannot be validated, or settings access fails.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate.
Use the documented 120 VAC outlets and power adapter for the scanner dock.
Stop if the dock does not light up after the documented power and cable connections are made.
Stop if the scanner does not charge to a green battery indication when placed on the dock.
access_or_tools_needed: Barcode Interface.exe installed on the laptop
FORTNA account login for the laptop
Network connectivity to AGV and Hospital control

access_required: FORTNA account login for the laptop
Access to Barcode Interface settings password
system_preconditions: Scanner has been replaced and requires setup.
Laptop network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control is validated by ping.
AGV control IP address is available.
Hospital control IP address is available.

steps.title: Gather setup requirements and verify network connectivity
steps.purpose: Ensure all required equipment, software, and network prerequisites are available before scanner setup begins.
steps.instruction: Gather a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe installed, a Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, and access to 120 VAC outlets. Confirm network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control by pinging their IP addresses, and ensure the AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses are available for later configuration.
steps.expected_result: All required hardware, software, power, and network prerequisites are available and connectivity to both control endpoints is validated.
steps.healthy_condition: Required equipment is present and laptop connectivity to both AGV and Hospital control is validated by ping.
steps.failure_condition: Required equipment is missing, network connectivity cannot be validated by ping, or required control IP addresses are not available.

actions.label: Confirm required equipment and software
actions.exact_action: Gather a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe installed, a Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, and 120 VAC outlets.

actions.label: Validate network connectivity
actions.exact_action: Validate network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control by pinging their IP addresses.

Scanner setup visuals associated with dock connection and connectivity preparation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control cannot be validated by ping.
Escalate if the required AGV control or Hospital control IP addresses are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a scanner is replaced, the new scanner needs to be setup.

Requirements include a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe, Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, 120 VAC outlets, and network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control validated by ping.

steps.title: Connect and power the scanner dock
steps.purpose: Establish the physical connection between the laptop, dock, and power source so the scanner can be configured.
steps.instruction: Connect the RJ45 side of the USB cable to the back of the scanner dock, connect the USB side to the laptop, connect the auxiliary lead to the power adapter, and plug the adapter into a 120 VAC outlet. Confirm the dock lights up.
steps.expected_result: The scanner dock is connected to the laptop and power, and the dock lights up.
steps.healthy_condition: The dock is powered and visibly lit after the documented connections are made.
steps.failure_condition: The dock does not light up after the documented cable and power connections are completed.

actions.label: Connect dock to laptop and power
actions.exact_action: Connect the RJ45 side of the USB cable to the back of the scanner dock, connect the USB side to the laptop, connect the auxiliary lead to the power adapter, and plug the adapter into a 120 VAC outlet.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Scanner dock connection points and setup arrangement.
Dock, cable, and power connection context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the dock does not light up after the documented power and cable connections are made.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Connect the RJ45 side of the USB cable to the back of the scanner dock, the USB to the laptop, and the auxiliary to the power adapter. The dock should light up.

steps.title: Charge the scanner if needed
steps.purpose: Ensure the replacement scanner has sufficient charge before configuration continues.
steps.instruction: If the scanner is uncharged, place it on the dock and wait until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.
steps.expected_result: The scanner battery indication changes from red to green while on the dock.
steps.healthy_condition: The battery symbol on top of the scanner turns green.
steps.failure_condition: The scanner does not reach a green battery indication while resting on the dock.

actions.label: Place scanner on dock
actions.exact_action: Place the scanner on the dock if it is uncharged.

actions.label: Verify battery indicator turns green
actions.exact_action: Wait until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Scanner and dock setup context related to charging state.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the scanner does not charge to a green battery indication when placed on the dock.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the scanner is uncharged it will need to rest on the dock until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.

steps.title: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account
steps.purpose: Gain access to the laptop environment required to run the Barcode Interface application.
steps.instruction: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account.
steps.expected_result: The laptop session is accessible under a FORTNA account.
steps.healthy_condition: The user is successfully logged into the laptop with the required account.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot log into the laptop with a FORTNA account.

actions.label: Log into laptop
actions.exact_action: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account. Launch the Barcode Interface application.

steps.title: Launch Barcode Interface
steps.purpose: Open the application used to verify connectivity and configure scanner settings.
steps.instruction: Launch the Barcode Interface application from the desktop.
steps.expected_result: The Barcode Interface application opens on the laptop.
steps.healthy_condition: Barcode Interface is open and available for scanner setup.
steps.failure_condition: Barcode Interface cannot be launched from the laptop.

actions.label: Open Barcode Interface
actions.exact_action: Launch the Barcode Interface application from the desktop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Barcode Interface application context associated with scanner setup.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account. Launch the Barcode Interface application.

steps.title: Check connectivity status and calibrate if needed
steps.purpose: Verify the scanner connectivity state in Barcode Interface and perform the documented calibration path only when required.
steps.instruction: Check the connectivity indicator at the bottom right of the screen. If it is green, continue. If it is red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.
steps.expected_result: The connectivity indicator is acceptable for continuing, either already green or addressed through the documented calibration flow.
steps.healthy_condition: The connectivity indicator is green, or calibration is completed per the onscreen instructions.
steps.failure_condition: The connectivity indicator remains red or the calibration process cannot be completed using the onscreen instructions.

actions.label: Check connectivity indicator
actions.exact_action: Check connectivity on the bottom right of the screen.

actions.label: Calibrate scanner if red
actions.exact_action: If red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Barcode Interface screen area associated with connectivity status and calibration.
Connectivity indicator and calibration-related screen context.
Scanner setup screen context for bottom-right connectivity indication.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Check connectivity on the bottom right of the screen. If green, skip this step. If red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.

steps.title: Open scanner settings
steps.purpose: Access the configuration area needed to verify prefixes and update endpoint URLs.
steps.instruction: Click the settings button and supply the password. By default, the password is "Opti."
steps.expected_result: The scanner settings screen is opened.
steps.healthy_condition: Settings are accessible after entering the password.
steps.failure_condition: Settings access is blocked or the documented default password does not work.

actions.label: Open settings
actions.exact_action: Click the settings button and supply the password.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Settings button and settings access screen.
Scanner settings screen associated with password-protected access.
Settings area used for scanner configuration.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if settings access is blocked and the documented password does not work.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Check the settings by clicking the settings button, supply the password. By default, the password is "Opti".

steps.title: Verify configured prefixes
steps.purpose: Confirm the scanner is configured to match the tote and location label scheme used by the system.
steps.instruction: Verify the configured prefixes match the labels on the totes and locations.
steps.expected_result: Configured prefixes match the tote and location labels.
steps.healthy_condition: Prefix values align with the labels on totes and locations.
steps.failure_condition: Configured prefixes do not match the tote and location labels.

actions.label: Verify prefixes
actions.exact_action: Verify prefixes match the labels on the totes and locations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Prefix values on the scanner settings screen for comparison to tote and location labels.
Settings fields related to prefix configuration.
Scanner configuration screen area used to verify prefixes.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Verify prefixes match the labels on the totes and locations.

steps.title: Update HTTP and OPC URLs
steps.purpose: Set the scanner communication endpoints to the documented AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses.
steps.instruction: Change the URL for the HTTP setting to contain the AGV control IP address and change the URL for the OPC setting to contain the Hospital control IP address respectively.
steps.expected_result: The HTTP URL contains the AGV control IP address and the OPC URL contains the Hospital control IP address.
steps.healthy_condition: Both URL fields contain the correct documented control IP addresses.
steps.failure_condition: One or both URL fields cannot be updated or the required IP addresses are unavailable.

actions.label: Update HTTP URL
actions.exact_action: Change the URL for the HTTP setting to contain the AGV control IP address.

actions.label: Update OPC URL
actions.exact_action: Change the URL for the OPC setting to contain the Hospital control IP address.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: HTTP and OPC URL fields on the scanner settings screen.
Configuration fields used to enter AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses.
Settings screen area related to URL configuration.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required AGV control or Hospital control IP addresses are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Change the URL for both the HTTP and OPC setting to contain the AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses respectively.

steps.title: Save scanner settings
steps.purpose: Persist the verified and updated scanner configuration.
steps.instruction: Save the configuration with the "Save Settings" button.
steps.healthy_condition: The updated configuration is saved successfully.
steps.failure_condition: The settings are not saved successfully.

actions.label: Save settings
actions.exact_action: Save the settings with the "Save Settings" button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The "Save Settings" button and surrounding configuration screen.
Settings screen used before saving configuration.
Configuration screen context for final save action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Save the settings with the "Save Settings" button.

success_criteria: The replacement scanner is connected and powered.
The dock lights up after the documented connections are made.
If the scanner was uncharged, the battery indication turns green on the dock.
Connectivity is acceptable or calibrated per the application.
Prefixes match tote and location labels.
HTTP and OPC URL settings contain the documented control IP addresses.
healthy_conditions: Required laptop, software, dock, cables, power, and network connectivity are available.
Scanner battery symbol turns green when charging is needed.
Connectivity indicator is green or calibration is completed per onscreen instructions.
Settings are accessible with the documented password.
Prefixes match tote and location labels.
HTTP and OPC settings contain the correct control IP addresses.
failure_conditions: Dock does not light up after the documented power and cable connections are made.
Scanner does not charge to a green battery indication when placed on the dock.
Network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control cannot be validated by ping.
Settings access is blocked and the documented password does not work.
Required AGV control or Hospital control IP addresses are not available.
Connectivity indicator remains red or calibration cannot be completed using the documented onscreen flow.
Prefixes do not match tote and location labels.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control cannot be validated by ping.
Escalate if settings access is blocked and the documented password does not work.
Escalate if the required AGV control or Hospital control IP addresses are not available.
Stop work if the dock does not light up after the documented power and cable connections are made.
Stop work if the scanner does not charge to a green battery indication when placed on the dock.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If a scanner is replaced, the new scanner needs to be setup.

Requirements include a Windows 10 or 11 laptop with Barcode Interface.exe, Zebra DS3608-SR scanner dock, USB cable, power adapter, 120 VAC outlets, and network connectivity to the AGV and Hospital control validated by ping.
Connect the RJ45 side of the USB cable to the back of the scanner dock, the USB to the laptop, and the auxiliary to the power adapter. The dock should light up.
If the scanner is uncharged it will need to rest on the dock until the red battery symbol shown on top turns green.
Log into the laptop with a FORTNA account. Launch the Barcode Interface application.
Check connectivity on the bottom right of the screen. If green, skip this step. If red, click "Calibrate Scanner" and follow the onscreen instructions.
Check the settings by clicking the settings button, supply the password. By default, the password is "Opti".
Verify prefixes match the labels on the totes and locations.
Change the URL for both the HTTP and OPC setting to contain the AGV control and Hospital control IP addresses respectively. Save the settings with the "Save Settings" button.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact ping commands or command syntax for validating connectivity.
The source does not provide exact AGV control or Hospital control IP address values.
The source does not provide estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
The source does not describe the exact calibration substeps beyond following onscreen instructions.
The packet includes some unrelated artifacts and context records that are not used as primary evidence for this scanner setup procedure.
